README.md Background section states: 'Web developers routinely benchmark competitor platforms and assess their own security posture using browser developer tools.' Task is framed as pre-deployment security assessment by a web developer

privileged_info_summary: The task audits HTTP security headers on 5 real, publicly accessible developer platforms: github.com, gitlab.com, bitbucket.org, npmjs.com, and pypi.org. All 5 sites serve HSTS (Strict-Transport-Security) headers with max-age values between 31,536,000 and 63,072,000 seconds. Four of five sites (all except npmjs.com) serve complete Content-Security-Policy headers with source directives. All five sites serve X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff. Four of five sites (all except npmjs.com) serve X-Frame-Options headers (deny or SAMEORIGIN). Total non-empty header values across all sites: 18 (4+4+4+2+4). The task requires minimum 3 headers per site and 10 total across all sites, both easily met by the actual data. Task file output is ~/Documents/security_audit_report.json. This is a realistic pre-deployment security assessment task for web developers.
